description: A great slicked-back hairdo will give you the highly coveted Clean Girl Aesthetic. But it will also pull back your skin to snatch it in all the right places. While your scalp and forehead may feel a little tight, the overall look is absolutely worth it.

Despite the heated side part vs. middle part debate, the besthair partreally is a personal choice. However, factors like face shape, hair type, and texture may influence your opinion. For instance, a side part or hair flip, as seen here on Reese Witherspoon, tends to complement round and heart-shaped faces, as well as those looking to add an instant boost of volume to hair.

Scarlett Johansson's tresses have always been blonde, but she's played around a lot with the length. In the last decade, though, she's clung to hermedium-length haircut in long layers. This helps her bangs frame her heart-shaped face—which, in turn, makes her seem more approachable and playful.

For many of us, the idea ofgetting bangsmay conjure up some cringe-worthy styles from childhood (uneven bowl bangs, anyone?). But there's something about wearing a set of fringe as an adult that feels refreshing. Here, Gemma Chan's choppy bangs provide the perfect accessory to her elegant bun.
Bobs are both timeless and trending, and can be customized to suit your hair type and face shape. For a unique spin on the cut, opt for a trendywet-look hair effectlike Jennifer Garner here.

A retro vibe supplies classic elegance. For proof, look no further than Halle Berry's 1940s-inspired red carpet hair. Created by celebrity stylistSara Seward, Berry's hair is a "deconstructedfinger wave" that covers one eye for a modern twist. The short crop, combined with wavy texture, plays up the hair's thickness.

Slicked-back hair is forever stylish. Case in point: Michelle Yeoh, whose swept-back, long, dark hair looks regal and elegant. Don't forget to apply a volumizing root-lifting powder and slightly bump up hair at the crown for a fuller-looking mane.

Elevate a classic ponytail like Kate Beckinsale's by giving it a loose wave at the end for movement and body. You can also give the top of your head a slight pouf for some added volume. Since it uses the existing hair to feign thickness, this style is especially great for those who may be experiencinghair thinning.

To keep natural hair strong and healthy, try aprotective style. Here, Missy Elliot gathers her mix of micro braids and twists into an intricate top knot half-up, half-down look with artistic edges to boot.

Shorter haircuts, like this chin-length bob on Kerry Washington, can give hair the appearance of fullness. Combined with luscious waves and a deep side part, it oozes Old Hollywood glamour. For a shiny, frizz-free finish, Noir recommends applyinghair oil.

There's nothing more youthful than a long, lustrous mane. Beyoncé's Botticelli-esque waves are defined with ribbons of golden highlights that had audiences fawning. Wavy texture will help hair to appear fuller, especially if it's thinning, andmoney piece highlightswill add even more movement.

Sofia Vergara's long hair is layered to frame her face and warmed with caramel highlights. If your hair is medium to thick in density, a lengthy mane will lend an air of exuberance. "You can wear your hair however you want as long as you have the tell-tale signs of youthful hair: fullness, shine, body, [and] bounce," says Lopez.

Simple yet stunning, Zoe Saldaña's collarbone-grazing lob had people stop mid-scroll. Created by celebrity stylist Mara Roszak, Saldana's cut is known as a "French bob." However, theAvataractress's style here has customized rounded ends that add a cool dose of polish and body.

A ponytail is a reliable style that is incredibly versatile. For a more casual vibe, make like Nicole Kidman and pull out a fewface-framing tendrils. To get a similar look, take about an inch of your hair from the front and loosely let it down to your cheeks.

It's unclear whether it's the ponytail, the flipped-out ends, or the bow ribbon (perhaps all of the above), but Jennifer Lopez's hairstyle exudes youth. As for her diamond jewelry? Pure sophistication. It's giving rich-girl vibes indeed.

If you love your length but are looking for a cut that adds shape and dimension to your hair, layers are the way to go. Arguably the queen of layers (the "Rachel"is iconic for a reason), Jennifer Aniston has sported face-framing layers for years and continues to look fabulous.

Celebrate your curlsin big, bold fashion. Angela Bassett wears her soft spirals voluminously, just past her shoulders. This length, combined with strategic layers, works to give her naturally curly hair shape and style.

Short and edgy, Taraji P. Henson's high fade is a classic. Her auburn highlights add a fun pop of color and dimension to her dark brown hair. To style this 'do, it's best to use a small amount of texturizing paste on your hair. Unsure how much paste to use? Start with a little and build your way up if necessary.

Simple doesn't mean boring. Give a classic bob some volume by bending the ends for body and bounce, as seen here on Naomi Watts. To give it a playful touch and show off your money maker, tuck the hair behind both ears.

Choosing between braids and leaving your curls loose? Why pick one when you can have both? In case you didn't know, Gabrielle Union's bohemiangoddess braidsare the full and textured protective style you need in your life.

Lobsare just as versatile as their shorter counterparts. Leslie Mann wears hers with slight waves to give her fine hair some body. This is a universally flattering haircut that works on all hair types and textures.

To style your braids in a simple yet striking way, try a multi-plaited ponytail look. Tracee Ellis Ross wears her hair slicked back into a high updo made of braided twists that go long past her shoulders. The result is fun, fresh, and oh-so stylish.

Cindy Crawford proves that no one wears the'90s blowoutbetter than the decade's own supermodels. This voluminous, shoulder-length cut with feathered ends is the picture of hair health—plus, its high volume helps make your face seem more narrow.

Add some movement to an ultra-short bob by giving it slight waves. Charlize Theron wears hers with a side part for added body. Apply a dose oftexturizing sprayto strands to land her cool and effortless style.

Andie MacDowell is a vision with her long and lustrous mane. The gray-to-silver color gradient creates natural dimension that even the most seasoned colorist couldn't imitate. Invest in a hair oil to land her next-level shine. And use a one-inchcurling ironto replicate the waves.

There's beauty in simplicity, and a buzz cut like Jada Pinkett Smith's makes a bold and powerful statement. Not only can it provide a solution to thinning hair, but it also demonstrates the cool confidence of its wearer.

These loose waves on Sandra Bullock's long, layered hair communicate a youthful, easy elegance. Use a curling iron to make styling hair easy and safe for your strands. To add a bit of volume to one side, you can opt for adeep side partinstead of a center part.

Viola Davis's Afro is a stunning example of the beauty of embracing your natural hair texture. Looking to don this look yourself? You'll need a pick and healthy coils. Tokeep curls hydratedand moisturized, be sure to use conditioning products.

Sometimes, retaining a youthful air means not fixing what's not broken. Though Gwyneth Paltrow has debutedmanydifferent cuts and styles, she's settled into her long, middle-parted, blonde tresses. If you're also going pin-straight, remember to keep your hair looking smooth and sleek with hair oil.

Not just any bob, Lucy Liu wears hers with a deep side part and blunt ends to elevated effect. The ends are rounded to soften the entire look and complement theCharlie's Angelsactress'ssquare face shape.

Who says adults can't wear pigtails? Priyanka Chopra Jonas gathers a small section of hair on each side before twisting them up into high pigtails. She keeps the rest of her long hair down for a more sophisticated interpretation of the fun hairstyle.

To create the appearance of a more lifted face, Lopez says you can cut your hair into layers that sit at the high points of your face, as seen here on Heidi Klum. The key is having a short layer that sits at the tail of the brow or at the end of your jaw. This will help add some symmetry to the face.

Lopez saysshorter cutscan keep your style fresh. So consider taking a page from Jamie Lee Curtis's playbook and opt for aclassic pixie cut. Bonus points if you lean into your natural color, as theFreakier Fridayactress does here.

Sometimes, a slicked-back updo can feel severe. For a more youthful, carefree hairstyle, follow Kate Hudson's lead and go for a looser 'do with face-framing tendrils. It's the definition of effortless elegance. Even better? Your head won't hurt from how tight your ponytail is.

trend_name: Eva Longoria Debuts Her Shortest Chop Since 2008 With a Gen Z-Approved Detail
description:
Longoria chopped off three inches just weeks after rocking a new butterfly cut.
Getty Images
Eva Longoriasubtly debuted a new bob haircut on Friday, and it may be her most dramatic hair transformation in decades. During a 50th birthday celebration for her friend and fellow actress Bonnie Boehmer, Longoria posed for a group pic with her face-framing bob front and center. "My girls!!!" Longoria captioned a photo shared to her Instagram Story on March 6.
The fresh chop comes just weeks after Longoria's hairstylist, Ken Paves, revealed her trendy new butterfly cut. "The Long and Short of it, Lovely Longoria," Paves captioned a photo dump of the actress. Longoria's golden-brown waves, which fell just past her shoulders in February, are now at least 3 inches shorter.
Eva Longoria/Instagram
Longoria let the sleek, middle-parted bob speak for itself, completing the look with natural makeup, a rosy pink lip, and a beige quarter-zip. From all angles, Longoria's bob shone, reminding us just how slick a bold bob can be. (Of course, we'll still be taking the choppy bob,the micro bob with bangs, and the classic, sophisticated bob into consideration all season long.)
Eva Longoria/Instagram
In October 2025, after years of sporting lengthy waves, Longoria teased her first step toward the dramatic chop. While in London to celebrate longtime friend Victoria Beckham's eponymous Netflix docuseries, theDesperate Housewivesalum rocked a face-framing lob, joining the dozens of celebrities who have tried on the chic hairstyle.
Of course, the voluminous lob was just a taste of what was to come from Longoria and her hairstyling team. Historically, the actress has been pretty consistent with her hairstyling choices. If the last few months are any indicator, it seems like Longoria has entered a new era of daring hair choices, and we are completely here for it.
With spring and summer inching closer, we can't wait to see what unexpected haircuts she surprises us with next.
Getty Images

trend_name: Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy Reportedly Requested a Major Hair Transformation Right Before Her Wedding to JFK Jr.
description:
The power couple tied the knot in September 1996.
Getty Images
Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy'ssignature hairstylemay be a focal point of Ryan Murphy'sLove Story, but it turns out the late fashion publicist wasn't always keen on remaining a blonde. Bessette-Kennedy's former hair stylist Brad Johns revealed as much in an exclusive interview withPage Six, during which he told the publication that she considered a major transformation just days before hertop-secret weddingtoJohn F. Kennedy Jr.
“She came in a nervous wreck and she said, ‘I have something very special coming up. Dye my hair black,'” Johns said. “I said, ‘Absolutely not.'”
Getty Images
The hairstylist added that he would "never" do such a dramatic dye job. “Women, when they’re in a panic, they want something different,” he explained.
Though Johns didn't actually know about the wedding at the time, he could tell she had “something special” planned. Thus, he defied her wishes entirely and added even more blonde to "frame her face."
Getty Images
“I said to her, ‘Thank God I left that those pieces in the front because you look like a princess,'” he remembered.
Getty Images
Bessette-Kennedy said "I do" opposite JFK Jr. on Cumberland Island, Georgia, shortly thereafter. The couple's guest list included a few dozen people, and once photos were released, her minimalist wedding gown basicallychanged the bridal industry forever. Three years later, the newlyweds tragically died in a plane crash off the coast of Martha's Vineyard along with Bessette-Kennedy's sister Lauren.

trend_name: Salma Hayek Proudly Embraces Her Grays While Rocking a Gen Z-Approved Hairstyle
description:
The actress, 59, has been open about her approach to aging over the years.
Getty
AsSalma Hayekhas shown on numerous occasions, she isn’t afraid to embrace her grays—in fact, she celebrates them.
Such was the case on February 15 when the actress spoke at Palacio Nacional in Mexico City, Mexico, where she and President Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo unveiled a series of initiatives to support local cinema and attract international productions to Mexico—the country Hayek hails from.
Getty
“There is no country in the world with such ecological diversity and beauty—here we have it all,” Hayek said, perThe Hollywood Reporter. “No other country has what we have.”
For the event on Sunday, Hayek wore a chocolate brown jacket atop a black top tucked into belted black pants, her brunette hair worn long, parted down the center—and, in a refreshing move, with a few grays visible. The 59-year-old has also let her grays shine through at events like Wimbledon in 2024, the Golden Globes in January 2025, and the Breakthrough Prize Awards in April of that same year.
Getty
In a September 2020 Instagram post, the actress called her gray hairs “the white hair of wisdom,” and in 2023,she cheekily shared a tipfor how “to cover your white hair without dyeing it,” she wrote alongside a photo of herself wearing sunglasses in her hair, which showed off her gray hair.
“Don’t wear your glasses on your hair!!!” she joked. “Bring on the wisdom.”
In an appearance onGood Morning America, Hayek wasn’t afraid to joke about her grays once more, telling the hosts, “You don’t see the white hair right now because I put mascara [on].”
“Because I’m too lazy to dye my hair, so I go like this and then I go like this,” she added, showing the audience the motion of not only putting mascara on her eyelashes but also on her hair framing her face.
Hayek has opened up over the years about howshe doesn’t believe in Botox, and how she’s embracing aging as she inches closer to 60—the age she’ll turn later this year. “I used to criticize myself so much,” Hayek previously toldPeople. “Now when I look in the mirror and there are things that I don’t see, I think about how much I’m going to like me in 10 years.”
Getty
“I’m an actress, a mother, and a wife—I want my expressions to be authentic,” she toldThe Cutof avoiding procedures like Botox. “I want my kids to look at my face and it’s very clear when I’m proud of them and clear when I’m not happy. I’m also an actress—I need the expressions on my face.”
Of aging, she toldGlamour, “I thought getting older meant I wasn’t going to work—I’m working. I thought getting older maybe meant that you’re not in love anymore—I’m in love.”
“I don’t feel that I lost my flexibility or my agility or even my strength,” she continued, adding of her husband François-Henri Pinault, “I do have to say that I have found it beautiful, getting older with someone.”
Getty
As for how Hollywood looks at aging these days, “Yeah, it’s changing,” Hayek said. “I was also told that, as an actress, you kind of stop working around 30. Imagine? I did really feel it at the time, and it’s unfair. If I had said instead to myself, ‘Don’t worry, your best professional moments are going to be in your 50s,’ I would have been devastated. ‘Oh my God, do I have to wait that long?’ But it’ll happen.”
Hayek added, “Hell, I’m still going. Shocker. I’m still here. They tried to get rid of me a thousand ways—I’m still here.”

Kieaundria Bellamyis a beauty and fashion writer atInStyleand a licensed aesthetician. With her 4B hair, she has an extensive black-owned hair care collection to keep her curls and coils flourishing. She leverages her writing and beauty expertise to uplift others, share honest reviews, and help women feel seen, heard, and celebrated in the beauty and fashion space.
A few bends of a large-barrel curling iron instantly make alobfeel dreamy and romantic. Paris Hilton’s sloping side part imbues her style with a touch of Old Hollywood glamour.
Easy, almost careless texture ensures that your hair reads soft and date-night-ready. This is doubly true for the bob. Balance the blunt cut with some artfully undone styling.
Tousled shagsare one of the defining hair trends of 2026. Halle Berry wears hers with a collarbone-skimming length, with sleek curtain bangs that call attention to her defined cheekbones.

Hairstylists agree that polished waves are an ideal Valentine’s Day look, and these generous bends are timelessly glamorous. To style, Fitzsimons suggests applying leave-in conditioner to damp hair before blow-drying. Once dry, separate lengths into two sections and use a curling wand to curl 2-inch sections away from the face. Finish with texturizing spray to lock in volume.
Tessa Thompson’swaterfall braidscreate a chic, slicked-back effect before they stream into lush, brushed-out curls. Tuck the style behind your ears to give it a casual, girl-next-door effect.
A high-slung ponytail reads more romantic when afforded some demure detailing. In this case, Ariana Grande takes her aesthetic cues fromBridgertonwith a soft sweep of baby bangs.

“Nothing says date night like a shiny, voluminous blowout,” says Papanikolas. The stylist suggests applying a shine-enhancing treatment to clean, damp hair before blow-drying with a round brush for a silken, high-shine finish.
A twist-up coupled with flirty tendrils (or, in this case, tendrils) is a V-Day mainstay. Make like Laura Harrier and opt for a single, wavy section pulled free to frame the face.

Natural curls and waves lend well to a fairytale-inspired updo. Pile lengths high on the head in a twist or loose bun that accentuates your texture—and be sure to loosen a quartet of tendrils to drive the princess point home.

Margaret Qualley’s finger-raked pony was made for Valentine’s Day. “I really adore a low pony for date night,” says Fitzsimons. “It is super easy to achieve, looks classy and sexy, and is a great option to keep your hair out of your face.” And while sleek ponytails have their place, subtle texture sets this style apart.

The business woman special, but make it romantic. A pinned updo featuring a side-swept element reads sophisticated and '90s, with a collective of tendrils (and Lily Collin's wispy bangs) softening the effect.
There’s something about a sculptural Old Hollywood wave that oozes coquettish glamour. Kendall Jenner’s blonde coiffure owes something to Lauren Bacall and Marilyn Monroe.

A simple curl reversal takes this look from flirty to sultry. Phoebe Dynevor’s deep part gives way to lengths curled in barrel fashion toward the face, the style effortlessly evoking the femme fatale archetype.
Weave some unexpected playfulness into your low-slung updo by trading your usual pony, knot, or bun for a looped and pinned plait.
There’s something eternally ethereal about extra-long hair. Allow your curls, waves, or pin-straight mane to cascade down your back, with secured sections at the front creating an Old World feel.

Chic and refined, the slicked knot is never off our roster. Consider this your get-out-of-styling-free card: just apply your preferred gel, styling cream, or oil, comb lengths into a pony, twist, and secure.

Experts agree that the low and polished pony is forever. After smoothing lengths with a flat-iron, the shaping begins. Fitzsimons suggests completing your styling routine with your preferred part in mind.
“To ensure it stays low, make sure to keep a portion of your ears covered,” he suggests. “Secure the ponytail with a hair tie and lift it slightly to give as much volume, and then tie it tightly.” For extra hold, add a spritz of hairspray to lock the style in place.
The buoyant blowout may be the moment, but a polished, understated version is a timeless take. Hair pulled into glossy, generous bends and curls offers a wash of elegance to any aesthetic.

Soft, could-be-natural waves are a date-night staple. “Romantic waves should have movement and dimension,” says Papanikolas. “Curl the hair in alternating sections, then rake through with your fingers for an undone, effortless feel.”

While many Valentine’s Day hairstyles center volume and bounce, flat-ironed lengths offer a luster that is just as striking. A deep side part ensures that the style gives glamour rather than pop star or bohemia.

TheFrench rollis back and eager to accompany you on your Valentine’s outing. The modern version of the confectionery and sculptural style can be dressed up or down, styled, and shaped to suit your aesthetic.
There’s nothing wrong with a bit of bedhead. Trading the recommended gloss and polish for air-dried texture that’s mussed and zhushed for an excess of effortlessness.
A low polished pony, but take it to the side. Nicole Kidman’s interpretation of the go-to updo is essentially styled as if the hair were to be worn loose, but softly secured at the last minute.
Gentle ringlets at the end of smoothed strands are a classic approach. In Nina Dobrev’s case, blunt bangs provide the style with a natural balance.

Valentine’s Day offers a welcome opportunity to get a bit regal. Pull inspiration from Felicity Jones with a subtle crown crafted from your own lengths. Simply separate two sections at the front and twist toward the back of the head, securing over loose length in tiara fashion or an updo for an all-out crown.
Yes, another blowout. This one, however, is supremely luxurious, offering a bridge between '90s supermodel and Old Hollywood with a perfect balance of gloss, undulation, and brushed curls.
When in doubt, rake your hair into a soft knot. Less is more with this natural look—your first attempt will likely be your best one.

trend_name: Chappell Roan Pairs a Plunging Victorian Corset With a Backless Thong-Bearing Skirt and High-Fashion Marge Simpson Hair
description:
A Tim Burton-esque spin on Victorian gothic chic.
Getty
Chappell Roanhas beentheone to watch in the front rows ofParis Fashion Weekthis year. Her latest look? I can only describe it as a sexy Victorian ghost woman from a Tim Burton-directed fever dream. And I mean that in the most complimentary way possible.
Getty
Making an appearance at Vivienne Westwood's show on March 7, the singer opted for a Victorian-esque gray corset dress, with a ruched plunging neckline, structured shoulders, and black beading details on the bodice. The long skirt featured a cutaway back, revealing her lacy black thong below. She completed the look with mesh black gloves and a black choker tied around her neck, and trailing down her back.
Getty
As ever, Roan's hair and makeup was a big part of the overall effect. Her extra pale, ghostly glam, courtesy of Andrew Dahling, featured gray eyeshadow, high-placed orange blush, and a mauve lip. As for her hair, it was styled in a towering curly updo that can only be described as high-fashion Marge Simpson.
Getty
This is only the latest in Roan's other-worldly, imaginative outings at Paris Fashion Week. For Acne's show, it was aspectral sheer gray lace corset dress with a faux curly bob. This was followed by a nude corset dress at Mugler. Then, alavender purple warrior princess dresswith a high ponytail for Rick Owens.
Honestly, Chappell Roan is making me feel like I'm living in a dark, fantastical fashion dreamland—long may it continue!
